note: Hand-edited config. Shards are sampled ONLY from `region` (normalised 0..1 box of the photo). For nuke-cloud this is a thin band along the horizon (y ~0.35 +/- a few px) spanning most of the width, so the trick is guessing WHERE along the horizon a shard belongs; `tolerance` gives vertical/horizontal leeway so it isn't bland. Other photos will want a different region. After editing, run `node scripts/generate-pieces.mjs` to resample targets + regenerate crops (writes worker/src/targets.json + public/pieces).
